recommended_action: Do not uproot — fragments regrow. Cut stems repeatedly, bag securely, dispose via municipal incineration. Report to canton.
Do not uproot — fragments regrow. Cut stems repeatedly, bag securely, dispose via municipal incineration. Report to canton.
Same as Japanese knotweed: do not uproot, cut + bag + incinerate, report to canton.
Same as Japanese knotweed: do not uproot, cut + bag + incinerate, report to canton.
Phototoxic sap — wear gloves, long sleeves, eye protection. Cut roots 10 cm below soil before flowering. Bag and incinerate. Report to canton.
Uproot before seed-set (before August). Plants compost safely if seeds not yet formed; bag and incinerate if flowering.
Saplings: pull entire root. Established trees: requires repeated cutting + herbicide — contact canton; sap is irritant.
Highly allergenic pollen. Wear gloves and mask, uproot whole plant before flowering, bag and incinerate. Mandatory reporting in CH.
Cut before flowering (June). Repeated mowing weakens stands; full eradication requires uprooting rhizomes.
Same as Canadian goldenrod: cut before flowering, repeated mowing, uproot rhizomes for full eradication.
Berries and leaves toxic. Uproot saplings, cut larger plants close to ground, monitor for regrowth.
Cut before seed-set (Sep). Uproot whole bush; remove flower heads to prevent seed dispersal.
Bark and seeds toxic. Saplings: pull whole root. Established trees: girdle + repeated cutting of suckers; contact canton.
Toxic to livestock. Uproot before flowering, bag and incinerate. Wear gloves.
Cut before flowering; uproot in small infestations to prevent spread.
Cut climbing stems at base; uproot to prevent regrowth from rhizomes.
Sap is irritant. Uproot saplings; established trees produce suckers — repeated cutting required.
Mandatory reporting in CH. Do not disturb soil — tubers spread. Contact canton for treatment plan.
Aquatic — do not transport fragments between waterbodies. Report to canton; clean boats and gear.
Aquatic — mandatory reporting. Do not handle without canton coordination; fragments regenerate.
